steps.title: Attach the appositive finding
steps.content: The phrase after the blank, a finding that, if true, would overturn current theories, renames and elaborates on the earlier evidence. Because that noun phrase is an appositive, it should be attached to the main clause with a comma.

steps.title: Reject boundary mistakes
steps.content: Choice A gives no punctuation before the appositive. Choice B turns the appositive into a sentence fragment. Choice D uses a semicolon before a noun phrase, but semicolons must separate independent clauses. The answer is C.
